tasks.name: Create activity type icon map helper
tasks.purpose: Map activity types to appropriate Lucide icons for visual differentiation in timeline

dependencies: lucide-react icons: BookOpen (lesson), Award (quiz), Presentation (presentation), ClipboardCheck (assessment)
constraints: Map each ActivityItem type to specific icon component
Export as const object for type safety
Add JSDoc explaining icon choices
Keep icon sizes consistent (h-5 w-5 by default)

tasks.input_state: ActivityItem type exists from S1918.I2.F1.T1 with discriminated union (type: 'lesson' | 'quiz' | 'presentation' | 'assessment')
tasks.output_state: Activity icon map exported as const with type-to-icon mapping for all 4 activity types
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Const object maps lesson→BookOpen, quiz→Award, presentation→Presentation, assessment→ClipboardCheck

tasks.name: Create activity-feed-widget component with timeline layout
tasks.purpose: Build timeline widget displaying last 5-8 activities with icons, descriptions, and relative timestamps

constraints: Server component (no 'use client' directive)
Receives ActivityItem[] as props (from S1918.I2.F3 loader)
Display max 8 activities (slice if needed)
Each activity row: icon, title/description, timestamp
Use formatDistanceToNow for relative timestamps with addSuffix
Activities are clickable links to relevant pages
Vertical timeline layout with subtle dividers
Icons use color variants per type (e.g., text-blue-500 for lessons)

tasks.input_state: Activity icon map exists from T1; ActivityItem type from S1918.I2.F1.T1
tasks.output_state: ActivityFeedWidget component exists with timeline rendering of activities
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Component displays activities with icons, descriptions, formatDistanceToNow timestamps, and links

tasks.name: Add empty state to activity-feed-widget
tasks.purpose: Handle case when user has no activities with helpful guidance and CTA

constraints: Show when activities array is empty (length === 0)
Display 'Your activity will appear here as you progress' message
Include 'Start Course' CTA as Button with link to /home/course
Use muted text for secondary message
Center empty state content in card
Maintain consistent spacing with populated state

tasks.input_state: Widget component exists from T2 without empty state handling
tasks.output_state: Widget has conditional rendering for empty state with message and Start Course CTA
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Widget shows 'Your activity will appear here' message when activities.length === 0 with Button link to /home/course
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'Your activity will appear here' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_components/activity-feed-widget.tsx && grep -q 'Start Course' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/_components/activity-feed-widget.tsx && pnpm typecheck

tasks.name: Wire activity-feed-widget to dashboard page
tasks.purpose: Integrate widget into dashboard page layout at correct grid position (left column, row 2)

patterns.expected_action: renders in dashboard grid with activities from loader

constraints: Import ActivityFeedWidget from _components
Place in left column, row 2 of grid
Pass recentActivity data from loader as activities prop
Grid slot should match layout from S1918.I1.F2

tasks.input_state: Dashboard page has grid layout; widget component exists from T2/T3; loadRecentActivity function exists from S1918.I2.F3.T9
tasks.output_state: Widget renders in dashboard page at left column, row 2 with loader-provided activity data
tasks.acceptance_criterion: ActivityFeedWidget imported and rendered in dashboard page.tsx at left column, row 2 with activities prop
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'import.*ActivityFeedWidget' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/page.tsx && grep -q '<ActivityFeedWidget' apps/web/app/home/\(user\)/page.tsx && pnpm typecheck

groups.name: Component Implementation
groups.description: Build timeline widget, add empty state, wire to page

critical_path.description: Icon map -> timeline component -> page integration

duration.notes: T2, T3, T4 cannot run in parallel due to sequential dependencies (T3 modifies T2 output, T4 depends on both)

validation.notes: All tasks are atomic (m=1), use single verbs, have clear outputs, and testable verification commands. Widget receives pre-aggregated data from S1918.I2.F3, so no database work required. Empty state and integration follow established patterns from Kanban Summary Widget.
